eventMetadata.context_description: Tensions between the Trump administration and Colombian President Gustavo Petro over narcotrafficking have fueled this market, with the US designating Colombia a "problem country" in its May 2026 National Drug Control Strategy, vowing legal, economic, and military pressure after cutting aid and intelligence sharing late last year. Recent US strikes on drug boats in the Pacific near Colombian waters—killing alleged traffickers but sparking sovereignty disputes—heighten risks, though a February joint commitment to target narco bosses and a February 4 Colombian airstrike on guerrillas signal cooperation. No direct US strike on Colombian soil has occurred in the past 30 days. Traders monitor Colombia's May 2026 presidential election, where a leadership change could ease bilateral frictions or prompt escalation.

markets.question: Will Argentina’s annual inflation in 2026 be between 30% and 34.9%?
markets.description:
This is a market about Argentinian inflation over the 12-month period ending December 2026, before seasonal adjustment, as reported by the National Institute of Statistics and Census (INDEC) of Argentina.

This market will resolve according to the percentage change in the Consumer Price Index (CPI / IPC) over the 12-month period ending in December 2026 (Variación % interanual Total nacional) according to the monthly INDEC report.

The resolution source for this market will be the INDEC Consumer Price Index report released for December 2026 (https://www.indec.gob.ar/), expected to be released in January 2027. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

You can find this report by clicking on the “Precios al consumidor” option on the home page of https://www.indec.gob.ar/, and searching the pdf for the figure under “Variación % interanual Total nacional”.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly INDEC CPI (IPC) news release which reports inflation over 12 month periods to only one decimal point (e.g. 33.6%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders price a tight race for Argentina's 2026 annual CPI inflation, with the 30-34.9% bin leading at 32.6% implied probability, closely trailed by 35-39.9% (25.6%) and 25-29.9% (24.5%), reflecting economist consensus around 30-35% amid disinflation under President Milei's fiscal austerity. March's 3.4% monthly print—the highest year-to-date—lifted year-on-year inflation to 32.6% and prompted the central bank's May REM survey to revise full-year forecasts up to 30.5%, aligning with IMF's 30.4% projection, due to energy and education cost pressures offsetting prior gains. Key swing factors include April CPI data due mid-May and sustained fiscal restraint versus global oil volatility; sustained sub-3% monthly readings could favor lower bins.

markets.question: Will Donald Trump publicly insult someone on May 14, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Donald Trump makes any public statement in which he insults, mocks, or attacks any non-fictional individual personally or professionally in a clearly negative manner on the specified date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". 

This includes calling the individual weak, stupid, disloyal, a failure, using an insulting nickname, using other derogatory language, or using the negative form of a positive trait in a derogatory personal way (e.g., “He/She isn’t smart”). Negative forms used in reference to the individual's professional actions, policies, or decisions (e.g., “He/She isn’t being smart about this policy”) will not count. Policy disagreements stated without disparaging language will not count.

A direct reference will qualify even if the individual is not named, so long as it is reasonably clear from context that they are the subject.

Any written, verbal, or recorded public statement by Trump qualifies.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Trump's longstanding pattern of direct, name-calling rhetoric in public statements, social media posts, press gaggles, and rallies drives trader consensus on markets tracking his insults. Over the past week, he insulted female reporters as "dumb" and "stupid" during a May 12 White House rope line exchange, while Team Trump labeled Nebraska Senate opponent Dan Osborn a "two-time loser" on X. On May 8, he was caught on microphone calling ABC's Rachel Scott a vulgar slur after a briefing. These follow April clashes labeling Pope Leo XIV "weak" over Iran policy calls. Upcoming White House remarks and rallies, per public schedules, heighten resolution risks amid ongoing foreign policy pressures.

markets.question: Will Applied Materials' Semiconductor Systems revenue be above $5.9B in Q2?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Applied Materials' Semiconductor Systems revenue for the second fiscal quarter of 2026, as reported in its official company earnings materials, is above the listed amount. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The specified metric will be considered as reported in the company’s official earnings materials. Subsequent revisions will not be considered.

If the specified company’s official earnings materials for the specified quarter are released, and the specified metric is not included, this market will resolve to “No”.

If the specified company does not release quarterly earnings materials for the specified quarter by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No”.

If the specified metric is reported as a range rather than a specific number, the midpoint of the range will be used for resolution of this market.

The resolution source for this market is Applied Materials' official company earnings materials, including press releases, investor presentations, and regulatory filings. If the specified metric is not reported in these materials, recordings or transcripts of the company’s earnings webcast may also be used.

Note: This market will resolve based on the most numerically precise version of the specified metric reported in the company’s official earnings materials. Only the specified metric will be considered; alternate versions that differ in definition or scope from the specified metric will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders on Polymarket price an implied 92% probability that Applied Materials' Semiconductor Systems revenue will exceed $5.7 billion in Q2 FY2026 (ending late April), anchored by the company's February guidance for total revenue of $7.65 billion (±$500 million) following Q1's $5.14 billion segment result amid surging AI chip demand. Accelerating investments in leading-edge logic, high-bandwidth memory, and advanced packaging—where Applied holds process equipment leadership—drove record DRAM revenue in Q1, with management forecasting over 20% semiconductor equipment growth in calendar 2026. New tools like Viva™ deposition and Sym3™ etch bolster competitive positioning against Lam Research and ASML. Q2 earnings on May 14 could refine outlook, with supply chain resilience key to avoiding surprises.

markets.question: Will Joe Flacco start Week 1 for the Chiefs in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed player who is the starting quarterback for the Kansas City Chiefs in their Week 1 regular season game of the 2026-2027 NFL season.

If the Chiefs do not name a single player as the starting quarterback, the market will resolve based on the player who takes the most snaps at quarterback during their first offensive drive of the game. If multiple players take the same number of snaps on the Chiefs’ first offensive drive, the market will resolve to the player who took the first snap at quarterback.

This market may not resolve before the start of the Chiefs’ Week 1 regular season game. 

The resolution sources for this market will be official information from the NFL (NFL.com); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices Patrick Mahomes as the Chiefs' Week 1 starting QB at 62% implied probability amid ongoing rehab from his torn ACL and LCL suffered in Week 15 of 2025 against the Chargers, with GM Brett Veach confirming on May 5 he's "way ahead of schedule" and eyeing OTAs participation, though typical nine-month timelines fuel doubt for the September opener versus Denver on MNF. The March trade acquiring Justin Fields from the Jets as cost-effective insurance—Chiefs paying just $3 million of his guaranteed salary—positions him next at 42%, bolstering depth behind an uncertain Mahomes. Gardner Minshew's 42.3% reflects his 2025 backup stint before signing a one-year Cardinals deal, while Joe Flacco (41.9%) and practice squad veteran Chris Oladokun (35.6%) linger as free-agent or internal alternatives given roster flux post-Minshew's exit and recent draft additions.

eventMetadata.context_description: Jannik Sinner's status as world No. 1 and blistering 2026 form, including a 33-2 record with recent Masters 1000 titles at Monte-Carlo and Madrid—capped by his straight-sets final win over Alexander Zverev—have solidified trader consensus at 53% implied probability for the 2026 Wimbledon men's title on grass. Carlos Alcaraz, a two-time recent champion with strong grass-court history, sits at 17.5% amid wrist injury concerns that forced withdrawals from Madrid and the French Open, raising doubts about his fitness for the grass swing. Novak Djokovic lingers at 7.8% on his seven Wimbledon triumphs and experience, despite age 39 and recent early exits like Rome's opener. Alexander Zverev's 3.8% reflects steady No. 3 ranking but consistent head-to-head losses to Sinner, while Americans Ben Shelton and Taylor Fritz draw niche support from grass prowess as dark horses.

markets.question: Will the lowest temperature in Hong Kong be 22°C on May 13?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the lowest temperature recorded by the Hong Kong Observatory in degrees Celsius on 13 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the Hong Kong Observatory, specifically the "Absolute Daily Min (deg. C)" the specified date once information is finalized in the relevant "Daily Extract", available here: https://www.weather.gov.hk/en/cis/climat.htm

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures in Celsius to one decimal place (eg, 9.1°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Hong Kong Observatory observations show the early morning low temperature on May 13 reached 26°C, with 7 a.m. readings at 27°C and current afternoon highs near 30°C amid sunny periods and light southwesterly winds, driving trader consensus to a 95.5% implied probability for 26°C as the daily minimum. This aligns with the latest 9-day forecast projecting 25–30°C ranges under a stable southerly airstream and anticyclone aloft, which limits nocturnal cooling in the humid subtropical climate typical of May, where historical lows average around 25°C but above-normal temperatures are expected this season. Realistic challenges include isolated heavy showers enhancing cloud cover for slightly more radiative cooling to 25°C, or an unforeseen wind shift, though model consensus indicates persistence through evening; final HKO daily summary expected post-midnight.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the MD-06 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the MD-06 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

Escalating primary ads and Trone's $11 million self-funding have intensified rhetoric over the past week but show no erosion in general election fundamentals, with Republicans' lesser-known primary field (Burnett, Ficker, Roca) offering little upset potential amid GOP's slim 217-212 House majority.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the tight Ligue 1 title race, PSG holds a six-point lead over second-place Lens ahead of their May 13 clash at Stade Bollaert-Delelis, where Lens boasts strong home form with 12 wins in 14 recent matches. PSG's trader-favored attack faces hurdles from multiple injuries—Achraf Hakimi, Nuno Mendes, Warren Zaïre-Emery, Willian Pacho, and goalkeeper Lucas Chevalier sidelined or doubtful—potentially limiting minutes and output for props on Dembele, Kvaratskhelia, or Ramos, while boosting usage for backups like Lee Kang-in or Fabian Ruiz. Lens counters with suspensions for Thomasson, Sangare, and Abdilhamid, plus injuries to Saint-Maximin and Thauvin, pressuring forwards like Wesley Said for goals or assists amid PSG's five straight head-to-head wins. Recent postponement drama underscores high stakes, with weather-neutral conditions expected.

markets.question: Will "Pinocchio" be said on ICEMAN?
markets.description:
Drake is expected to release his new album "ICEMAN" in 2026.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed term is mentioned by anyone on the specified transcript for any track on the officially released full "ICEMAN" album. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

Officially released means that Iceman is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date. Any Drake album officially confirmed to be the Iceman project will count, regardless of potential name changes.

Samples or clips of audio where people are speaking or singing which are included in the album will qualify toward this market's resolution so long as it is included in the specified transcript. AI-generated audio will count toward this market's resolution so long as it is included in the specified transcript.

In the case a term is censored on a relevant transcript, audio may be consulted to verify that a qualifying mention occurred.

Misspellings or iterations of the listed term, including all grammatical or slang forms, or misspellings with extra, missing, or incorrect letters (ex: helloooooooo or heoll, for ‘hello’), will not count toward a “Yes” resolution, regardless of context or intent.

Any usage of the term, regardless of context, will count toward the resolution of this market.

Plural and possessive forms of the listed term will count toward the resolution of this market regardless of context; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

This market will resolve according to the initial release of the full album on a qualifying streaming platform. Releases consisting only of individual songs or remixes released later will not qualify.

The resolution source for this market will be the audio transcripts for this album provided by the listed streaming platforms in the following order of priority: Spotify, Apple Music, YouTube Music, Amazon Music, Tidal (e.g., Spotify will be used unless it does not provide a necessary transcript, then Apple Music will be used, etc.).

If no such album is released in full by by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to based on what has been released up to that point. If the album is not released on any of the listed platforms by the specified end date, this market will resolve to "No".

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed actor playing the listed character appears in Avengers: Doomsday. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". 

This market will resolve upon announcement of casting, regardless of if the film is delayed, cancelled, or otherwise fails to be released. If no casting is announced, this market will resolve upon footage of the domestic theatrical cut of the film. If multiple people are cast for the same role, the listed individual being among the cast list for the relevant character will be sufficient to qualify this market towards a "Yes" resolution.

If no casting is announced and Avengers: Doomsday is definitively cancelled or fails to be released by June 30, 2027, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

This market will resolve based on official announcements from Marvel or Warner Bros., the domestic theatrical cut of Avengers: Doomsday, or a consensus of credible reporting.

markets.question: Will EUR/USD hit 1.35 (High) in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Investing.com high price (“H”) for any EUR/USD hourly candle for an hour on or before the listed end date (ET) is equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Data for a given candle will be considered finalized once the next candle appears on the specified graph. The last trading day of a given week will be considered finalized once the market closes on that day, typically at 5 PM ET on Friday.

This market will resolve as soon as any finalized EUR/USD hourly candle high price is equal to or above the listed price, or once the final hourly candle in the specified period is finalized. A candle starting at 11:00 PM ET on a given date will be considered to be on that date.

This market’s resolution will be based solely on information from the “H” figure located at the top of the EUR/USD Streaming Chart on Investing.com for the specified currency pair (e.g., https://www.investing.com/currencies/eur-usd-chart).

Mallorca's Vedat Muriqi (21 goals, Pichichi contender) remains a props focal point after scoring in their 1-1 Villarreal draw, but Getafe's home defense and low-scoring H2H trends (three 1-goal margins in last five) temper expectations in this tight late-season encounter.

markets.question: Will Orinats Yerkir win the most seats in the 2026 Armenian National Assembly election?

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the Armenian National Assembly in this election.

If voting in the Armenian parliamentary election does not occur by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party or coalition which received a greater number of valid votes. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Armenian government, specifically the Armenian Central Election Commission (https://www.elections.am/).

markets.question: Counter-Strike: SINQU vs KUUSAMO.gg (BO3) - European Pro League Regular Group C
markets.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Elimination match between SINQU and KUUSAMO.gg in the European Pro League Regular Group C, initially scheduled for May 11 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "SINQU" if SINQU win the match against KUUSAMO.gg.

This market will resolve to "KUUSAMO.gg" if KUUSAMO.gg win the match against SINQU.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: In European Pro League Regular Season 5 Group C's lower bracket BO3, SINQU (#238 HLTV) faces KUUSAMO.gg (#213) in a must-win Finnish matchup for playoff contention, with both at 0-1 after opening losses—SINQU routed 0-2 by ALGO (6-26 maps) and KUUSAMO downed 0-2 by BIG Academy. markets.question: Will Doja Cat have a #1 song on the Billboard Hot 100 in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any song by the listed artist is the number 1 song in any official weekly Billboard Hot 100 songs chart released for a data collection reference period entirely between January 1 and December 31, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Any song released with the listed artist as a primary artist will count, including songs released before 2026. If a song is released jointly by multiple artists credited equally as primary artists, it will count for all of them. Features, writing credits, production credits, or other forms of non-primary contributions to a song will not count. Artist attribution will be determined solely by the artists displayed by Billboard on the relevant chart.

Billboard updates its Hot 100 songs chart each Tuesday (with adjusted release schedules on some holiday weeks), reflecting data collected in the previous week (Friday-Thursday). Each Billboard chart is then titled “Week of (date of the upcoming Saturday)”. The first Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will be the chart titled “Week of January 17, 2026” for a data collection reference period of January 2-8, 2026. The final Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will be the Chart titled “Week of January 9, 2027” for a data collection reference period of December 25-31, 2026.

Updates or revisions made within this market’s timeframe to the #1-ranked song on previously published Billboard Hot 100 charts will be considered; however, such updates will not disqualify a previously published Billboard Hot 100 #1 song from counting. If a Billboard Hot 100 #1 song is revised after initial publication, both the originally published #1 song and the revised #1 song will count for this market. Updates or revisions made after the release of the final Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be the weekly Billboard Hot 100 songs charts published each week at https://www.billboard.com/charts/hot-100/ and through other official Billboard channels.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the NY-25 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NY-25 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Jakarta be 37°C on May 14?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Halim Perdanakusuma Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 14 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Halim Perdanakusuma Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/id/jakarta/WIHH.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a 46% implied probability for Jakarta's highest temperature reaching exactly 35°C on May 14 at Halim Perdanakusuma International Airport station—the official resolution source—driven by recent observations of near-35°C peaks in early May amid dry season onset, minimal cloud cover, and urban heat island amplification in the densely built metro area. BMKG's hourly forecast for central Jakarta (Gambir) projects a daytime maximum of 32°C under mostly clear conditions with light northerly winds and 64-81% humidity, but airport tarmac and subsidence-induced radiative heating often yield 2-3°C higher readings during similar May setups, where historical averages hover at 32°C yet spikes to 35°C occur on 20-30% of days. Strengthening Australian monsoon risks afternoon showers (51% chance per models), potentially capping intensity, with final Wunderground data expected post-midnight May 15 to resolve amid inherent forecast uncertainty.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket, with 31.5% implied probability for 33°C and 28.5% for 32°C, reflects the inherent uncertainty in short-range forecasts for Lagos amid the onset of Nigeria's wet season. NiMet and global models like GFS and ECMWF project highs near 32°C under partly cloudy skies with moderate sea breeze moderation from the Atlantic, high humidity above 80%, and scattered thunderstorms capping peak temperatures. Differentiating factors include morning cloud clearance—favoring 33°C with prolonged insolation—and early convective activity or stronger onshore flow, tilting toward 31°C or below; historical mid-May averages hover at 31.5°C, amplified slightly by urban heat island effects. Final NiMet bulletins today and hourly airport observations tomorrow will sharpen resolution.

markets.question: Will Baidu have the best AI model at the end of May 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the highest arena rank among primarily Chinese companies, based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on May 31, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control off will be used to resolve this market.

Qualifying Chinese Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie still remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Meituan” would be ranked ahead of “Xiaomi”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies first place under this ranking.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

This market resolves according to the finalized match status as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
Any match where an official result is declared — including results decided by DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, a Super Over, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling — will resolve Yes. (Draws count as completed results when recognized by the competition.)
If the match is abandoned, ends in No Result, is permanently canceled, or otherwise concludes without an official result, the market will resolve No.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed or permanently canceled, after which it will resolve as described above.

events.title: T20 World Cup, East Asia-Pacific Qualifier, Regional Final: Cook Islands vs Vanuatu

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the London City Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 14 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the London City Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/gb/london/EGLC.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

events.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the London City Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 14 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the London City Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/gb/london/EGLC.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket closely splits between 13°C (35%) and 12°C (32%) for London City Airport's highest temperature on May 14, driven by the Met Office's latest forecast of a maximum 14°C amid bright spells, blustery westerly winds gusting to 23 mph, and occasional heavy showers with hail risk. These conditions—patchy cloud suppressing solar insolation and wind enhancing mixing of cooler boundary-layer air—differentiate outcomes, as greater shower coverage or persistence could cap peaks at 12°C via evaporative cooling, while clearer intervals support 13-14°C. Below May norms of 17°C due to lingering low-pressure trough, expect intraday Met Office updates to refine guidance before resolution.

markets.question: Will Donald Trump visit Japan in 2026?
markets.description:
If U.S. President Donald Trump visits a listed country between January 1 and December 31, 2026 11:59 PM ET, the relevant market will resolve to "Yes". Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purpose of this market, a "visit" is defined as Trump physically entering the terrestrial or maritime territory of the listed country. Whether or not Trump enters the country's airspace during the timeframe of this market will have no bearing on a positive resolution.

The primary resolution source for this information will be official information from government of the United States of America, official information from Trump or released by his verified social media accounts (e.g. https://twitter.com/POTUS), however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: **President Donald Trump's confirmed 2026 international visits—Switzerland for the January Davos World Economic Forum and China for a May 14-15 state visit with Xi Jinping, delayed from March amid U.S. focus on Iran tensions—anchor trader assessments of diplomatic priorities like trade talks and Middle East de-escalation.** This week's Chinese foreign ministry confirmation elevates bilateral summit prospects, while earlier Davos remarks highlighted NATO strains and Greenland discussions. Upcoming G7 summit in France (June 15-17) and NATO summit in Turkey (July 7-8) offer potential additional trips, though midterm election barnstorming and G20 hosting in Miami may limit further travel before December 31 resolution, emphasizing physical territorial entry per market rules.

markets.question: Ukraine coup attempt by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a widely reported coup attempt in Ukraine at any point between market creation and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

A coup attempt is defined as a coordinated and deliberate effort by military, security forces, or other state actors (or factions thereof) to overthrow or unlawfully seize control of the Ukrainian government or its leadership. Revolutionary actions by non-state forces, isolated protests, or general unrest will not alone qualify.

Claims by the Ukrainian government that a coup attempt was foiled—such as through arrests or disruption of a planned operation—will only count toward a "Yes" resolution if credible independent sources widely report and explicitly characterize the event as a coup attempt. Foiled plots or arrests that occur without any attempted execution of the coup will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects overwhelming stability in Ukraine's government, with no credible reports of coup plots or military disloyalty emerging in the past 30 days amid the ongoing Russian invasion. President Zelenskyy maintains firm control, bolstered by unified command structures and Western support, despite isolated anti-mobilization protests in cities like Odessa that have not escalated to systemic threats. Recent European intelligence has instead highlighted Kremlin paranoia over potential internal coups against Putin, including fears of elite-driven assassinations, diverting attention from Kyiv. Absent verifiable catalysts like high-level arrests, defections, or no-confidence signals before June 30, traders price negligible risk of a successful challenge to Zelenskyy's authority.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors "No" at 95% for Les Wexner facing criminal charges by June 30, driven by the absence of any DOJ indictment or active prosecution despite intense scrutiny. February 2026 Epstein file releases unredacted 2019 FBI memos naming Wexner a co-conspirator alongside Ghislaine Maxwell, spurring his House Oversight Committee deposition where he denied wrongdoing and claimed Epstein defrauded him. April civil lawsuits by Epstein victims allege Wexner enabled crimes via funding, yet these remain distinct from criminal proceedings. With no recent federal announcements or court filings signaling imminent charges, traders anticipate barriers like statutes of limitations and evidentiary hurdles persisting.

markets.question: Games Total: O/U 2.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Round of 16 match between KOLESIE and TDK in the CCT Europe Series 1 Playoffs, initially scheduled for May 13 at 10:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Over" if KOLESIE and TDK play 3 or more maps in this series.

If fewer than 3 maps are played, this market will resolve to "Under".

Maps won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the total, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching map being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

events.title: Counter-Strike: KOLESIE vs TDK (BO3) - CCT Europe Series 1 Playoffs
events.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Round of 16 match between KOLESIE and TDK in the CCT Europe Series 1 Playoffs, initially scheduled for May 13 at 10:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "KOLESIE" if KOLESIE win the match against TDK.

This market will resolve to "TDK" if TDK win the match against KOLESIE.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: TDK carries momentum into the CCT Europe Series 1 playoffs Round of 16 BO3 against KOLESIE after a 3-1 group stage run, featuring 2-0 shutouts over Famalicão and GenOne plus a 2-1 win against Lavked on May 10, offsetting an opening loss to INOX Division. KOLESIE, a direct playoff invite at HLTV #71 (ahead of TDK's #86), shows mixed form with recent losses to Walczaki (1-2, April 29) and SINNERS (0-2, April 26), though bolstered by April roster additions fr3nd and ex1st. No head-to-head history exists; TDK excels on Overpass (81% win rate) and Dust2 (69%), contrasting KOLESIE's strengths on Ancient (68%) and Nuke (73%), shaping trader consensus on map vetoes and stylistic edges.

markets.question: Will the next governing coalition of New Zealand include National + Labour?
markets.description:
A general election is scheduled to be held in New Zealand on November 7, 2026.

This market will resolve to the first ruling coalition of parties that forms after the next New Zealand general election.

A party will only be considered part of the ruling coalition if it participates in the governing coalition and provides at least one Cabinet minister. Parties that merely support the government from outside Cabinet, including through confidence-and-supply agreements or similar arrangements, without holding a Cabinet post will not qualify.

The following parties will be considered for this market: ACT New Zealand (ACT), Labour Party (Labour), Te Pāti Māori (Maori), National Party (National), Green Party (Green), and New Zealand First Party (NZF). All other parties will not be considered.

Resolution will be based on the listed option that most completely matches the parties included in the coalition. If the coalition formed after the election includes all of the parties listed in a market option, along with any other parties, that option will resolve to “Yes” unless there exists another market option that more completely covers the coalition. A listed option will not resolve to “Yes” if any of its listed parties are not included in the coalition.

For example:

- If the governing coalition includes Labour + Green + ACT, but no option explicitly lists those three together, the market will resolve to “Labour + Green” if that option is listed and no listed option more completely matches the coalition.

- If the governing coalition includes National + ACT + NZF, the option “National + ACT + NZF” will resolve to “Yes”, while “National + ACT” will resolve to “No”.

In the event that the ruling coalition includes all parties for multiple market options, and each option contains an equal number of coalition parties, the tie will be resolved in favor of the option whose listed parties together hold the greater number of seats in the newly elected House of Representatives. If this presents another tie, this market will resolve in favor of the listed option whose parties received the greatest number of valid party votes.

If any party forms a single-party majority government, the listed option for that party will be considered the governing coalition.

If the governing coalition does not include all parties in any listed option, this market will resolve to “Other”.

This market may resolve once the first government is officially confirmed following the appointment of the Prime Minister and ministers by the Governor-General after the 2026 New Zealand general election, with ministers appointed under non-caretaker circumstances.

If the next New Zealand ruling coalition after the election is not confirmed by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

This market will resolve based on a consensus of credible reporting. In case of ambiguity, this market will resolve based on official information from the government of New Zealand.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the WTA 125 Parma Ladies Open on clay, third seed Dayana Yastremska (#52) advanced to the round of 16 with a 7-6(3), 6-3 win over qualifier Victoria Bosio on Tuesday, snapping a three-match losing streak amid a 8-12 record this season. Unseeded Hanne Vandewinkel (#98), riding strong 2026 form with a 23-8 ledger and recent career-high ranking near 92, defeated Whitney Osuigwe 7-5, 6-4 in her opener. No prior head-to-head exists between the power-hitting Ukrainian and the surging Belgian; trader consensus likely reflects Yastremska's superior ranking and experience against Vandewinkel's momentum and clay-court upside, with no reported injuries or withdrawals ahead of Wednesday's matchup.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: BetBoom Team and paiN Gaming meet in the IEM Atlanta 2026 Group A upper bracket final BO3, with the winner advancing directly to playoffs semi-finals and the loser dropping to quarter-finals. BetBoom carries massive momentum from yesterday's 2-1 upset over world No. 1 Vitality on Anubis (13-10), Overpass (8-13), and Nuke (13-8), fueled by Magnojez's 1.26 rating and zorte's AWPing amid recent roster tweaks with FL4MUS. paiN rides an eight-match win streak, clinching playoffs 2-1 over FUT on Nuke (13-8), Overpass (10-13), and Dust2 (13-11), building on their 2-0 opener win versus FaZe; piriajr and snow shone brightly. paiN leads head-to-head 2-0 from January's BLAST Bounty, but map vetoes loom large—BetBoom thrives on Nuke (88% win rate) and Ancient, while paiN prefers Inferno and holds edges on Overpass and Dust2.

markets.question: Will Z.ai have the #3 AI model at the end of May 2026 (Style Control On)?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the third-highest arena rank (Style Control On) based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on May 31, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control on will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies third place under this ranking system.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices the United Kingdom at 35% and Austria at 27.5% implied probability for last place in the Eurovision 2026 grand final, driven by underwhelming second rehearsal clips released this week that exposed lackluster stagings for frontrunners. The UK's experimental electronic entry "Eins, Zwei, Drei" by Look Mum No Computer draws niche appeal with its gadgetry but faces backlash for gimmicky vibes amid the nation's dismal recent history, including perennial bottom finishes. Host Austria's Cosmó with "Tanzschein" disappoints despite home Vienna crowd potential, slammed by vocal coaches and fans as the contest's weakest despite jury sympathy upside. Belgium's Essyla ("Dancing on the Ice"), fresh from semi-final 1 qualification yesterday, and Germany's Sarah Engels ("Fire") trail with generic pop lacking momentum. Second semi-final qualifiers tomorrow and dress rehearsals could reshape the field before Saturday's jury-televote showdown.

eventMetadata.context_description: Manchester City command an 86% implied probability for second place in the Premier League table, trailing leaders Arsenal by five points with two matchweeks left but holding a substantial gap over third-placed Manchester United. Arsenal's recent 3-0 thrashing of Fulham and victory over West Ham extended their lead, yet mounting injury concerns around Ben White and Riccardo Calafiori introduce vulnerability in their run-in against Burnley and Crystal Palace. City, buoyed by Ruben Dias' return from hamstring absence, face winnable home fixture versus Crystal Palace today before Bournemouth away, fueling trader consensus on securing runners-up spot and Champions League qualification. Realistic challenges include City overtaking Arsenal via maximum points and Gunners' slip-ups, or an unlikely collapse allowing United or Liverpool to close the standings gap.

markets.question: Will Movimento Democrático Brasileiro (MDB) win the most seats in the next Brazilian Senate election?
markets.description:

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the next Brazilian Senate election.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors the Partido Liberal (PL) at 77% to win the most seats in Brazil's October 4, 2026, Senate election, where 54 of 81 seats are contested amid high stakes for checking executive and judicial power. Recent Genial/Quaest polls from late April 2026 across major states like Rio de Janeiro (Cláudio Castro leading), Paraná (Filipe Barros competitive), and Minas Gerais (Domingos Sávio vying for second) underscore PL's edge, bolstered by opposition momentum from the Senate's April 29 rejection of President Lula's Supreme Court nominee and ongoing STF tensions. Already the largest Senate party with 16 seats via recent defections, PL benefits from Bolsonaro allies and projections estimating 24-30 total seats post-election. Centrists like MDB (Simone Tebet topping São Paulo) and Republicanos trail as challengers, while PT lags outside the Northeast. Candidate filings by August could refine dynamics.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Paris be 17°C or higher on May 14?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Paris-Le Bourget Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 14 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Paris-Le Bourget Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/fr/bonneuil-en-france/LFPB.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a razor-thin contest between 12°C (40%) and 13°C (36%) for Paris's highest temperature on May 14, driven by Météo-France's latest guidance forecasting afternoon highs of 13–14°C under persistent overcast skies and 90% shower probability from a polar maritime air mass entrenched since May 10. Northwest winds of 16–48 km/h and 5–9 mm expected precipitation sharply limit diurnal heating, 5–8°C below May norms of ~18°C, with 11°C (17%) capturing downside risk from thicker cloud decks in high-resolution AROME model runs. Ensemble spreads across ECMWF and GFS reflect uncertainty in exact cloud breaks and shower timing, with final observations resolving via official Paris station data tomorrow.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket assigns near-even implied probabilities to 64-65°F (32%) and 62-63°F (29%) for New York City's highest temperature at LaGuardia Airport on May 14, reflecting genuine uncertainty in National Weather Service (NWS) guidance and GFS/ECMWF ensemble means clustering around 63-65°F amid a persistent cool upper-level trough over the Northeast. This feature has suppressed highs below the mid-70s seasonal average through mid-May 2026, with light southerly winds providing modest warm air advection but offset by lingering mid-level clouds limiting peak afternoon heating. Differentiating factors include model spread on cloud cover timing—ECMWF leans cooler with thicker overcast, while GFS shows more breaks—and exact trough position; new 12Z model runs and NWS forecast discussions expected later today could tip the balance ahead of tomorrow's resolution.

markets.question: Will Citigroup fail by June 30, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed bank fails between market creation and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

For the purposes of this market, the listed bank will be considered to have “failed” if, within the listed date range, any of the following occurs under the bank’s applicable legal or regulatory framework:

- The listed bank’s primary banking regulator formally declares the institution insolvent or non-viable, or withdraws or revokes the bank’s license or authorization, and such determination initiates or directly results in resolution, liquidation, wind-down, or transfer actions.
- The listed bank enters a court-ordered liquidation, statutory resolution regime, or regulator-mandated wind-down, including the use of resolution tools such as bail-ins, forced asset transfers, or the establishment of a bridge bank.
- A government or resolution authority intervenes in a manner that wipes out or subordinates existing equity of the listed bank and transfers effective control of the bank to the state or a designated resolution authority, with continued operations dependent on official intervention.
- The listed bank publicly defaults on a payment obligation, including derivatives margin, repo, or physical commodity delivery, and such default is formally acknowledged by the bank’s primary regulator or resolution authority and directly results in the initiation of resolution, liquidation, license withdrawal, or regulator-mandated transfer of the bank.
- The listed bank is subject to a compulsory merger, acquisition, or transfer of all or substantially all of its assets and liabilities ordered or directed by its primary banking regulator or resolution authority due to the bank’s financial condition or to prevent failure, regardless of whether a formal insolvency declaration or immediate equity wipeout is publicly announced at the time of transfer.

If there is a potential failure of the listed bank within this market’s date range and a qualifying regulatory or court action has occurred but has not yet been fully published by the relevant authority, this market may remain open  to allow for confirmation. If no qualifying failure is confirmed by that date, this market will resolve to “No.”

The primary resolution source for this market will be official statements, filings, or actions by the listed bank’s primary banking regulator or resolution authority; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

- The listed bank is subject to a compulsory merger, acquisition, or transfer of all or substantially all of its assets and liabilities ordered or directed by its primary banking regulator or resolution authority due to the bank’s financial condition or to prevent failure, regardless of whether a formal insolvency declaration or immediate equity wipeout is publicly announced at the time of transfer.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects negligible failure risk for tracked institutions by June 30, bolstered by large banks' robust capital positions affirmed in the Federal Reserve's February 2026 supervisory stress tests under severe recession scenarios. Year-to-date, solely two small failures occurred—Metropolitan Capital Bank & Trust ($261 million assets, Chicago, closed January 30) and Community Bank & Trust-West Georgia ($288 million assets, LaGrange, closed May 1)—attributable to localized credit concentrations and resolved swiftly by FDIC purchase-and-assumption deals with minimal Deposit Insurance Fund losses. Commercial real estate delinquency rates climbed to 4.02% in Q1 2026 amid office sector weakness, but banks' proactive provisioning and portfolio sales have curbed systemic spillover. Q2 earnings post-deadline will reveal updated metrics.

markets.question: Will Prosperous Armenia win the most seats in the 2026 Armenian National Assembly election?
markets.description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Armenia on June 7, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the Armenian National Assembly in this election.

If voting in the Armenian parliamentary election does not occur by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party or coalition which received a greater number of valid votes. In the event that results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party or coalition whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party or coalition in the Armenian National Assembly. 

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Armenian government, specifically the Armenian Central Election Commission (https://www.elections.am/).

markets.question: Will Elon Musk and Sam Altman settle?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the claims between Elon Musk and Sam Altman in Musk v. Altman et al., Case No. 4:24-cv-04722-YGR (U.S. District Court, Northern District of California), are settled by 11:59 PM ET on December 31, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A settlement refers to an agreement between Elon Musk and Sam Altman that resolves all claims between them in this case, whether through a formal settlement agreement, stipulated dismissal, or other court-recognized resolution indicating that the claims between these two parties have been resolved. Announcements of such a settlement will qualify, regardless of whether final paperwork is completed or court approval occurs after the deadline.

Settlements involving other parties (e.g., OpenAI or any additional defendants) will not count unless they also resolve all claims between Elon Musk and Sam Altman.

If all claims between Elon Musk and Sam Altman are resolved through a final court judgment (e.g., trial verdict or dispositive ruling), or through a unilateral dismissal of claims not constituting a settlement, before any qualifying settlement occurs, this market will immediately resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source will be official information from the U.S. court system (including filings, orders, or docket updates). A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Barcelona lead La Liga standings by 11 points over Real Madrid entering matchday 37, hosting fifth-placed Real Betis at Spotify Camp Nou on May 17 in a fixture with title implications for the hosts and European qualification stakes for the visitors. Barca's dominance is tempered by an injury crisis, with star winger Lamine Yamal sidelined for the season via hamstring tear, though Raphinha and Andreas Christensen recently resumed full training ahead of their May 3 Osasuna clash; doubts linger over Christensen's partial ACL recovery. Betis boast solid away form after stunning Real Madrid in late April but face absences including defender Marc Bartra (foot, late May) and Junior Firpo (muscle). Historical head-to-head favors Barcelona (30 wins in 46 meetings), with home advantage and superior goal difference underscoring trader focus on squad depth and motivation.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices the United Kingdom at 35% and Austria at 27.5% implied probability for last place in the Eurovision 2026 grand final, driven by underwhelming second rehearsal clips released this week that exposed lackluster stagings for frontrunners. The UK's experimental electronic entry "Eins, Zwei, Drei" by Look Mum No Computer draws niche appeal with its gadgetry but faces backlash for gimmicky vibes amid the nation's dismal recent history, including perennial bottom finishes. Host Austria's Cosmó with "Tanzschein" disappoints despite home Vienna crowd potential, slammed by vocal coaches and fans as the contest's weakest despite jury sympathy upside. Belgium's Essyla ("Dancing on the Ice"), fresh from semi-final 1 qualification yesterday, and Germany's Sarah Engels ("Fire") trail with generic pop lacking momentum. Second semi-final qualifiers tomorrow and dress rehearsals could reshape the field before Saturday's jury-televote showdown.

markets.question: Will Starmer say "Liz" or "Truss" during the next Prime Minister's Questions event?
markets.description:
Keir Starmer is scheduled to participate in the Prime Minister's Questions (PMQs) in the Commons Chamber in the Palace of Westminster on every Wednesday. (https://www.parliament.uk/visiting/visiting-and-tours/watch-committees-and-debates/prime-ministers-questions/).

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Keir Starmer says the listed term during the next Prime Minister's Question Time event he participates in. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

Any usage of the term regardless of context will count toward the resolution of this market.

Pluralization/possessive of the term will count toward the resolution of this market, however other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

AI-generated audio or video will not count toward this market's resolution.

If no such Prime Minister's Question Time event happens by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No". If Starmer ceases to be Prime Minister for any length of time by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

This market is explicitly about scheduled events featuring Keir Starmer titled "Prime Minister's Question Time" (https://www.parallelparliament.co.uk/mp/keir-starmer/dept/cabinet-office/calendar). Speeches, events, or comments that occur outside of named, scheduled events will not qualify toward this market's resolution. If the event contains a Q&A, it will count toward the resolution of this market.

The resolution source will be video of the events. Only remarks which are broadcast or streamed live will count toward this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Labour's heavy losses in recent local elections have intensified internal party pressure on Prime Minister Keir Starmer, with several ministers resigning and over 80 MPs demanding his departure, prompting trader focus on his defensive posture at the next Prime Minister's Questions on May 20. Expect opposition Leader Kemi Badenoch or Reform representatives to grill him on party unity, poor results, and scandals like Mandelson, with Starmer likely countering via government achievements in workers' rights and child poverty initiatives. State Opening today sets legislative tone ahead of this key parliamentary clash.

Six Republicans and one independent fragment the GOP vote, raising the possibility both Democrats advance under the top-two system, as occurred in recent cycles amid vote-splitting. Early voting began May 4 amid redrawn district lines from Proposition 50 that narrowed but preserved Democratic favoritism. No public polling exists, leaving trader consensus focused on fundraising edges, endorsements like the California Democratic Party for Thompson and Our Revolution for Jones, and turnout in this solidly blue but competitive primary landscape.

Differentiating factors include morning precipitation timing—persistent rain favors 21–22°C, while afternoon clearing could push toward 23°C+—amid model spreads of 23–26°C and historical rainy-day averages 3°C below May's 28°C norm. Evening forecast updates from CMA may shift sentiment before official station resolution.

markets.question: Set Handicap: Jodar (-1.5) vs Darderi (+1.5)
markets.description:

This market will resolve to "Jodar" if Rafael Jodar wins by 2 or more sets than Luciano Darderi, based on the final completed score. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Darderi."

events.title: Internazionali BNL d'Italia: Rafael Jodar vs Luciano Darderi
events.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Rafael Jodar and Luciano Darderi in the Internazionali BNL d'Italia, originally scheduled for May 13, 2026 at 7:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Rafael Jodar' if Rafael Jodar advances against Luciano Darderi.

This market will resolve to 'Luciano Darderi' if Luciano Darderi advances against Rafael Jodar.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rising 19-year-old Spaniard Rafael Jodar (ATP #34, career-high reached May 4) advanced to the Internazionali BNL d'Italia quarterfinals with a gritty 6-1, 4-6, 6-3 win over Matteo Arnaldi, extending his strong 2026 form (19-9 record) on clay. He faces local favorite Luciano Darderi (ATP #20), who produced the tournament's biggest upset yesterday, saving four match points to stun world No. 3 Alexander Zverev 1-6, 7-6(10), 6-0 after earlier beating Tommy Paul. In their first head-to-head, Darderi's superior clay pedigree (51-24 career) and Foro Italico crowd boost offset potential fatigue from his grueling marathon, while Jodar's fresh momentum and youth fuel trader interest in this best-of-three-sets ATP Masters 1000 showdown.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a razor-thin split between 24°C (32.5%) and 25°C (27.5%) as Shanghai's May 14 high temperature, reflecting the latest short-range guidance from GFS, ECMWF ensembles, and China Meteorological Administration models converging on 24-26°C amid partly cloudy conditions and easterly breezes at 10-13 mph. These winds advect cooler maritime air, capping daytime heating despite typical May climatological highs near 25°C, while passing shower risks (20-25%) add uncertainty to solar insolation and peak hourly temperatures measured at official stations like Xujiahui. Recent observations on May 13 show overcast skies holding highs below 27°C, tempering upside potential; watch afternoon CMA updates and evening soundings for shifts in boundary layer stability that could tip the balance.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the NY-16 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NY-16 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

markets.question: Will Jerome Powell depart as Fed Chair between June 20 and June 26?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed date range during which Jerome Powell vacates his role as Chair of the Federal Reserve Board of Governors.

Vacating his role refers to Powell actually ceasing to hold the position of Chair of the Federal Reserve Board of Governors. Announcements of Powell's resignation or firing will not alone qualify.

The scheduled end of Powell’s term as Chair will not alone qualify. If Powell continues to serve as Chair on a temporary basis (e.g. until the confirmation of his successor), he will not be considered to have vacated his role as Chair.

This market is restricted to Powell’s status as Chair of the Federal Reserve Board of Governors. Powell’s status as a regular member of the Federal Reserve Board of Governors will not affect this market’s resolution.

All dates refer to the Eastern Time zone (ET).

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Jerome Powell and the Federal Reserve; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

Vacating his role refers to Powell actually ceasing to hold the position of Chair of the Federal Reserve Board of Governors. Announcements of Powell's resignation or firing will not alone qualify.

The scheduled end of Powell’s term as Chair will not alone qualify. If Powell continues to serve as Chair on a temporary basis (e.g. until the confirmation of his successor), he will not be considered to have vacated his role as Chair.

This market is restricted to Powell’s status as Chair of the Federal Reserve Board of Governors. Powell’s status as a regular member of the Federal Reserve Board of Governors will not affect this market’s resolution.

All dates refer to the Eastern Time zone (ET).

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Jerome Powell and the Federal Reserve; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Jerome Powell's term as Federal Reserve Chair expires May 15, 2026, and his April 29 public confirmation that he will step aside precisely then—while remaining on the Board of Governors until 2028—anchors trader consensus at 93.9% for departure in the May 15–22 window. Recent Senate action advanced President Trump's nominee Kevin Warsh for Chair on May 11 (49-44 vote with Democratic crossovers) and confirmed him to the Fed Board on May 12 (51-45), signaling a seamless transition despite prior holds like Sen. Tillis's opposition resolving via DOJ probe closure. Powell's board stay breaks 75-year precedent amid Trump threats of removal, but Chair exit appears locked. Challenges include Senate confirmation delays for Warsh prompting interim extension, unexpected legal disputes over term end, or late health/legal developments.

markets.question: Will the UK designate the IRGC a terrorist organization by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United Kingdom government formally designates the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) as a terrorist organization by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

This includes designation as a proscribed terrorist group or organisation according to the UK Home Office, as well as the use of Royal Prerogative Powers or Orders in Council, legislation signed into law, or the creation of a new national designation that formally designates the IRGC as a terrorist organization.

Statements by government officials, including the Prime Minister, Home Office, or National Crime Service, will not count unless accompanied by a formal designation.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting or official information from the UK Government.
